1.21.1.0.504.0:
### 0.504.0

**Villagers trade anything — new required mod: Trade Everything**

Every villager now has a Trade Anything slot at the top of its trade list. Put any item in and the villager exchanges it for goods it buys, priced by real material worth — crafting and smithing recipes, durability, and enchantments all count. Valuable items pay out emeralds or emerald blocks, and emerald blocks break into emeralds automatically when you buy things, with change returned. Powered by a new companion mod, Trade Everything, bundled inside Dungeon Train.

- Sell junk or treasure to any villager — prices follow material value (16 common items ≈ 1 emerald)
- Enchantments and durability change what your gear is worth
- Emerald blocks work as big currency: broken only when needed, change given in emeralds
- Fully configurable in config/tradeeverything.json

**Villager trades scale with the train — beyond Master**

Train villagers now key entirely off their own carriage: deeper carriages host higher-level traders (Novice near spawn, Master from ~carriage 520), and from carriage 30 the gear they sell keeps growing past vanilla — better enchants every ~100 carriages, stats slightly ahead of the local difficulty, and prices that scale with the enchants (big-ticket items charge emerald blocks). Villagers also buy their own stock back at 10% under their sell price. NOTE: the exchange rates and scaling curves are first-pass and still under balancing review — expect tuning in upcoming releases, and please report trades that feel off.

- Deeper carriages = better traders: levels, enchants, stats and prices all follow the carriage
- Enchants keep improving past vanilla Master (every ~100 carriages from carriage 30)
- Sell items back to their seller at a 10% margin
- Balancing is under active review — feedback welcome

### 0.503.0

**Fixed multi-second freezes caused by dropped items near the train**

Items and mobs colliding with parts of the train whose terrain hadn't generated yet could lock the whole game up for many seconds (frozen trading, unresponsive menus, hanging quit). Collision checks no longer force that terrain to generate on the spot.

- No more total freezes when items pile up on or fall off a moving train
- Rare cosmetic side effect: an item dropped onto a not-yet-generated part of the train may fall through instead of landing

### 0.502.0

**New advancement: Cover me in ...leather?**

Wear a full set of leather armor whose rolled stats beat a full vanilla diamond set. Deep-train leather can out-roll diamond — this challenge advancement rewards finding out.

- New challenge advancement in the Dungeon Train tab, under Dungeon Train Explorer
- Earnable once loot rolls scale up — realistically around the first Nether band

### 0.501.0

**Changed Adventure Item Stats settings now switch the world to Free Play**

Dungeon Train's balance is tuned against Adventure Item Stats' standard settings. If those settings have been changed, the world now runs in Free Play for the session — advancements and stats don't count toward your profile while the change is in place, and everything returns to normal once the standard settings are back. On joining you're told exactly which settings differ, and one click on the chat notice restores the defaults for you, backing up your old settings file first.

- Worlds with modified Adventure Item Stats settings run in Free Play (session-only — restoring the settings restores normal play)
- The join notice lists exactly which settings were changed
- One-click /fixaisconfig restores the standard settings, backing up your old file first

### 0.500.0

**Support-funnel usage telemetry + Finances dashboard**

The title screen's Support the Mod, Discord and Patreon buttons — and every link on the Support page — now report anonymous usage counts (clicks, whether the external link was actually opened, and time spent on the page) when network features are consented to. Nothing new is shown in game; the data feeds a new Revenue section on the dt-data explorer's Finances page.

- Consent-gated: no network consent, no telemetry
- Tracks clicks, confirm-screen follow-through and time on the Support page
- Data explorer Costs page relabelled Finances with a Revenue funnel
1.21.1.0.499.0:
### 0.499.0

**AI-translation ring in the language list**

Each Dungeon Train-translated language in the language menu now wears a blue ring around the DT logo: the filled part of the circle shows how much of that translation is AI-generated without human review. A full ring is pure machine translation; it empties as human translators review lines — Simplified Chinese, the most human-reviewed locale, shows only a thin sliver.

- Blue arc = AI-unreviewed share, drawn clockwise from 12 o'clock over a faint full-circle track
- Same 35% blue as the existing AI label; human-reviewed locales keep their solid logo

### 0.498.0

**Per-line translation credits, behind the scenes**

Every translated line in all 19 languages now records who produced it — which AI model, or which community translator — and who has human-reviewed it. This is repo-side bookkeeping (nothing changes in-game): it makes unreviewed machine translation easy to find for review passes, and properly credits 老本願's Chinese translation work and 阿世xAsh's Support-page revisions line by line.
1.21.1.0.497.0:
### 0.497.0

**Old worlds clean themselves up when you reboard**

Dungeon Train has always been one world per ride — now your save folder agrees. Reboarding after death discards the old world instead of saving it: the run's world is deleted and a fresh one takes its place, so your world list and disk stay clean. A little trash icon next to the reboard button lets you turn this off if you'd rather keep your fallen worlds, and renamed or hand-made worlds are never touched. As the old world slips away, the train sees you off with a rotating farewell line — Chinese players get a few classical sayings of their own.

- Reboarding deletes the old run's world (on by default) — toggle it with the trash icon on the death screen
- Only auto-generated run worlds are cleaned up; renamed, hand-made and editor worlds are always kept
- New 'Leaving the world behind…' farewell lines during the switch, with classical sayings for Chinese players
1.21.1.0.496.0:
### 0.496.0

**Chinese Support-page copy, revised by a community translator**

The 'Support the Mod' page's Simplified and Traditional Chinese text has been rewritten by a community translator for a much more natural read. The page also now words itself correctly for your install: modpack players see modpack wording, mod-only players see mod wording.

- All 15 Support-page strings revised in both 简体中文 and 繁體中文
- Copy adapts automatically between mod and modpack installs

### 0.495.0

**Same seed, same world — for real this time**

A follow-up to the seed fix: the last places the mod itself made same-seed worlds differ are now deterministic. Trackside stairs land in the same spots with the same left/right rhythm every time, corridor cleanup always finishes before your world saves, and dungeon chest loot rolls identically for identical seeds.

- Trackside stairs and underground stair shafts now place deterministically (roughly every 100 blocks, alternating sides)
- Corridor cleanup finishes at shutdown, so saved worlds are always fully swept
- Chest loot in track and tunnel structures is now seed-reproducible
1.21.1.0.494.0:
### 0.494.0

**Book voting in your language**

The train's vote page now speaks all 19 supported languages — its questions, the (Y)es/(N)o labels, and every chat reply it gives after you vote. Until now the vote page always appeared in English, whatever language you play in.

### 0.493.0

**Smoother ticks in the upside-down region**

Removed a hidden per-tick cost in the upside-down region: the server was repeatedly re-checking mirrored-terrain chunks that couldn't be processed yet. Those chunks now wait quietly until they're actually ready, cutting that background overhead roughly five-fold.
1.21.1.0.492.0:
### 0.492.0

**Faster world generation between special zones**

The train's world generator now skips its special-zone terrain work (nether mountains, biome forcing, track-bed checks) on chunks that are provably outside those zones — most of the ride. Chunk streaming in the overworld stretches costs roughly a third less server time, so long rides stay smoother, with terrain byte-identical to before.

- Overworld gaps between zones generate ~25-40% faster; upside-down band ~38% faster
- Terrain and biomes are unchanged everywhere — existing worlds continue seamlessly

### 0.491.0

**Same seed, same world: band layout is now seed-deterministic**

Creating a world with a fixed seed now always produces the same nether-band mountains, biome front, and band layout — previously each fresh world rolled its own internal layout seed even with the seed box filled in. The band terrain system also now activates before the very first spawn chunks generate, so early chunks can no longer miss band biomes, and any biome-override error is now logged instead of silently baking the wrong biome. Existing worlds are unaffected.

- Fixed-seed worlds now reproduce the same band terrain and biome front
- Band biomes can no longer be missing from the first-generated spawn chunks
- Silent biome-fallback errors are now logged

### 0.490.0

**AI label on machine-translated languages**

The language-selection list now marks Dungeon Train translations that have not been human-reviewed with a small faded blue AI label next to the Dungeon Train logo, so you can tell at a glance which translations are machine-generated.

- Human-reviewed languages keep the solid logo with no label

### 0.489.0

**Vote on the books other players wrote**

Community books now end with a page from the train itself: it asks whether you enjoyed the book, with thumbs up / thumbs down (or just press Y / N from any page). Voting closes the book, the train answers in chat, and the burn takes your side — green flames for a yes, red for a no, with the usual orange still flickering through. Votes travel back to the rails and quietly shape how often that book is found by other travellers.

- A dimmed, train-voiced vote page appears after the last page of player-written books
- Y / N hotkeys vote instantly from any page
- Green / red tinted burns for voted books — Death Notes keep their soul burn
- Popular books surface more often in loot, panned ones ride quieter

**Found books always burn again**

Fixed a wedge where chest books could get stuck as their built-in stand-in when the community pool had nothing new to offer — they now resolve properly (repeats allowed) and always burn after reading, like any other book of the train.
1.21.1.0.488.0:
### 0.488.0

**Official links now stay up to date**

The Discord invite, Patreon, donation and hosting-affiliate links now update themselves from the Dungeon Train server whenever you're online, so buttons in the menus always point at the current official destinations — even on older versions of the mod. Offline, the built-in links still work as before.

- Links refresh once per session from the title screen; no account data is sent
- Default donation amount is now $25 AUD (was $45)
1.21.1.0.487.0:
### 0.487.0

**In-game update notifications**

Dungeon Train now tells you inside the game when a newer version is available — an "Update available" badge appears next to the mod on the Mods screen. It only notifies; it never downloads or changes anything on its own, so you stay in control and update through your launcher as usual.

- "Update available" badge on the Mods screen when you're behind the latest release
- Notification only — no auto-download; update via CurseForge / Modrinth / your launcher
